purpose: Publish bounded readiness states for the main public audience profiles so outside readers can see what this repository is ready for today, which prerequisites apply, and which adoption paths remain intentionally deferred.

profiles.summary: External reviewers can evaluate maturity, scope, strongest evidence, and bounded limits without reading the private runtime repository.
can_do_now: verify repo-local evidence and release posture
prerequisites: treat PUBLIC_EVIDENCE_STRENGTH_MAP.json and PUBLIC_RELEASE_METADATA.json as stronger than convenience summaries
treat PUBLIC_FRESHNESS_MODEL.json as the bounded stale-summary and refresh-trigger guide for reviewer-facing summaries
bounded_non_goals: does not expose the private N1Hub runtime
does not claim a hosted public service

profiles.summary: Integrators can build against the published validator-facing HTTP, schema, and example surfaces today.
can_do_now: generate or handcraft validator requests from public contracts
use API examples and client recipes
prerequisites: use the published OpenAPI and envelope schemas
treat live validator behavior as stronger for edge-case runtime semantics
bounded_non_goals: does not promise a public hosted endpoint
does not replace live validator behavior for edge cases

profiles.summary: Contributors can propose bounded public-surface changes today when they stay inside the published docs, schemas, examples, and reviewer/release layers.
can_do_now: open issues for docs, examples, contract questions, and bounded change proposals
prepare pull requests against the public projection surface
follow explicit reviewer and release sync rules
prerequisites: keep release-facing and reviewer-facing summary layers synchronized
bounded_non_goals: does not grant access to private upstream runtime surfaces
does not permit widening the public boundary by accident

profiles.summary: Tool-builders can consume the public schemas, OpenAPI, and cataloged machine-readable artifacts today.
can_do_now: generate tooling from public schemas and OpenAPI
map examples to contract surfaces without guessing
prerequisites: use PUBLIC_CONTRACT_CATALOG.json and PUBLIC_EVIDENCE_STRENGTH_MAP.json to choose stronger generation targets
treat summary JSON layers as subordinate to schemas, OpenAPI, and release evidence
bounded_non_goals: does not guarantee a complete SDK
does not expose private generator internals

profiles.summary: Maintainers can keep reviewer, release, governance, and adoption summary layers synchronized as the public repo evolves.
can_do_now: update public release evidence and review layers coherently
reclassify summary layers when stronger-source hierarchy changes
prerequisites: refresh PUBLIC_ADOPTION_READINESS.json whenever audience readiness, prerequisites, or deferred scope changes
refresh PUBLIC_FRESHNESS_MODEL.json whenever reviewer shortcuts, release evidence, or stale-summary triggers change
bounded_non_goals: does not expose private maintainer playbooks
does not replace stronger provenance and release evidence

profiles.summary: This public specs repository is not itself a hosted service surface and should not be adopted as if it exposed a public production validator endpoint.
can_do_now: review the bounded limits and no-lock-in posture
understand which hosted-runtime assumptions are intentionally out of scope here
prerequisites: do not treat examples or client recipes as evidence of a guaranteed hosted endpoint
bounded_non_goals: does not publish a production hosted validator
does not promise service uptime, auth provisioning, or runtime tenancy features
